{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260716012233ee6998ccff4a486e","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_40360674acb24144a7ced5be","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/error_response_validation.go","content":"package server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR RESPONSE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides helper functions for validating and inspecting HTTP error\n// responses. These helpers support common error testing scenarios by providing\n// convenient functions to check status codes, content types, error codes, and\n// error message content.\n//\n// All helpers in this file are designed to work with S3-compatible error\n// responses in both XML and JSON formats.\n//\n// Usage:\n//   assertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\n//   errorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n//   if hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") { ... }\n//   assertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"not found\")\n// =============================================================================\n\n// assertErrorResponse validates that an HTTP response is an error response with\n// the expected status code and content type.\n//\n// This helper performs basic validation of error responses by checking:\n// - The HTTP status code matches the expected value\n// - The Content-Type header contains the expected type\n// - The status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx range)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 403, 500)\n//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type substring (e.g., \"application/xml\", \"application/json\")\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tassertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\nfunc assertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedContentType string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Validate status code\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content type (allow partial match for charset parameters)\n\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, expectedContentType) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected content type to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure status code indicates an error response\n\tif resp.StatusCode < 400 || resp.StatusCode >= 600 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code %d does not indicate an error response (expected 4xx or 5xx)\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n}\n\n// parseErrorResponse extracts error details from an HTTP response body.\n//\n// This helper reads the response body and attempts to parse error details from\n// either JSON or XML format. It supports both formats for maximum flexibility:\n// - JSON: {\"code\": \"...\", \"message\": \"...\"}\n// - XML: <Error><Code>...</Code><Message>...</Message></Error>\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error details from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - errorCode: The parsed error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//   - message: The parsed error message\n//   - err: Error if parsing fails or body cannot be read\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\terrorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tif errorCode != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", errorCode)\n//\t}\n//\tt.Logf(\"Error message: %s\", message)\nfunc parseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (errorCode string, message string, err error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Read response body\n\tbody, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif readErr != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", readErr)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t// Try JSON format first\n\tvar jsonError struct {\n\t\tCode    string `json:\"code\"`\n\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\t}\n\tif jsonUnmarshalErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &jsonError); jsonUnmarshalErr == nil && jsonError.Code != \"\" {\n\t\treturn jsonError.Code, jsonError.Message, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Try XML format (S3 standard)\n\tvar xmlError S3Error\n\tif xmlUnmarshalErr := xml.Unmarshal(body, &xmlError); xmlUnmarshalErr == nil && xmlError.Code != \"\" {\n\t\treturn xmlError.Code, xmlError.Message, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// If neither format works, return error\n\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response as JSON or XML (body: %s)\", string(body))\n}\n\n// hasErrorCode checks if a response contains a specific error code.\n//\n// This helper parses the error response and checks if it contains the expected\n// error code. It returns true if the error code matches, false otherwise. This is\n// useful for conditional validation and error type checking in tests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedErrorCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\", \"InvalidRequest\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if the response contains the expected error code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tif !hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n//\t    t.Error(\"Expected NoSuchKey error code\")\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Conditional logic based on error type\n//\tif hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"AccessDenied\") {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Authentication failed as expected\")\n//\t} else if hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Resource not found as expected\")\n//\t}\nfunc hasErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\terrorCode, _, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn errorCode == expectedErrorCode\n}\n\n// assertErrorMessageContains validates that an error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper checks that the error message in the response contains the expected\n// text substring. It performs a case-sensitive substring match, allowing for\n// flexible error message validation without requiring exact matches.\n//\n// This is useful for validating error message content when the exact wording\n// may vary, but key information should always be present.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedText: Text that should be present in the error message (case-sensitive)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"not found\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"does not exist\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"key\")\nfunc assertErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t_, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ADDITIONAL ERROR RESPONSE HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// Additional helper functions for common error response validation scenarios.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// getHTTPStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from a response.\n//\n// This is a simple helper that returns the status code. Use it when you need\n// to check the status code in assertions or conditional logic.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract status code from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - int: The HTTP status code\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tstatusCode := getHTTPStatusCode(resp)\n//\tif statusCode != 404 {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected 404, got %d\", statusCode)\n//\t}\nfunc getHTTPStatusCode(resp *http.Response) int {\n\treturn resp.StatusCode\n}\n\n// getContentType extracts the Content-Type header from a response.\n//\n// This helper returns the Content-Type header value. Use it when you need\n// to check the content type in assertions or conditional logic.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract Content-Type from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - string: The Content-Type header value (empty string if not present)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tcontentType := getContentType(resp)\n//\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, \"application/xml\") {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected XML content type, got '%s'\", contentType)\n//\t}\nfunc getContentType(resp *http.Response) string {\n\treturn resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n}\n\n// getResponseBody reads and returns the response body as a string.\n//\n// This helper reads the entire response body and returns it as a string.\n// The response body is consumed in the process, so this should only be called\n// once per response. Use it for debugging or when you need to inspect the raw\n// response content.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to read body from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - string: The response body content\n//   - error: Error if reading the body fails\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tbody, err := getResponseBody(t, resp)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tt.Logf(\"Response body: %s\", body)\nfunc getResponseBody(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (string, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\treturn string(body), nil\n}\n\n// isErrorStatusCode checks if a status code indicates an error.\n//\n// This helper returns true if the status code is in the 4xx or 5xx range,\n// indicating an error response. Use it for conditional logic based on whether\n// a response indicates success or failure.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to check\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if status code is 4xx or 5xx, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tif isErrorStatusCode(resp.StatusCode) {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Request failed as expected\")\n//\t}\nfunc isErrorStatusCode(statusCode int) bool {\n\treturn statusCode >= 400 && statusCode < 600\n}\n\n// isClientError checks if a status code indicates a client error (4xx).\n//\n// This helper returns true if the status code is in the 4xx range, indicating\n// a client error (e.g., bad request, unauthorized, not found).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to check\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if status code is 4xx, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tif isClientError(resp.StatusCode) {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Client error (4xx) as expected\")\n//\t}\nfunc isClientError(statusCode int) bool {\n\treturn statusCode >= 400 && statusCode < 500\n}\n\n// isServerError checks if a status code indicates a server error (5xx).\n//\n// This helper returns true if the status code is in the 5xx range, indicating\n// a server error (e.g., internal server error, service unavailable).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to check\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if status code is 5xx, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/trigger-error\")\n//\tif isServerError(resp.StatusCode) {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Server error (5xx) as expected\")\n//\t}\nfunc isServerError(statusCode int) bool {\n\treturn statusCode >= 500 && statusCode < 600\n}\n\n// isSuccessStatusCode checks if a status code indicates success (2xx or 3xx).\n//\n// This helper returns true if the status code is in the 2xx or 3xx range,\n// indicating a successful response.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - statusCode: HTTP status code to check\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if status code is 2xx or 3xx, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/existing-key\")\n//\tif isSuccessStatusCode(resp.StatusCode) {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Request succeeded\")\n//\t}\nfunc isSuccessStatusCode(statusCode int) bool {\n\treturn (statusCode >= 200 && statusCode < 300) || (statusCode >= 300 && statusCode < 400)\n}\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"53cef9e1-8a43-45ce-a6f5-4617a5109218","uuid":"b1135155-08e6-4a65-9adb-1be3db5a8427"}

This is\n// useful for conditional validation and error type checking in tests.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedErrorCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\", \"InvalidRequest\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if the response contains the expected error code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tif !hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n//\t    t.Error(\"Expected NoSuchKey error code\")\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Conditional logic based on error type\n//\tif hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"AccessDenied\") {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Authentication failed as expected\")\n//\t} else if hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n//\t    t.Log(\"Resource not found as expected\")\n//\t}\nfunc hasErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\terrorCode, _, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn errorCode == expectedErrorCode\n}\n\n// assertErrorMessageContains validates that an error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper checks that the error message in the response contains the expected\n// text substring. These helpers support common error testing scenarios by providing\n// convenient functions to check status codes, content types, error codes, and\n// error message content.\n//\n// All helpers in this file are designed to work with S3-compatible error\n// responses in both XML and JSON formats.\n//\n// Usage:\n//   assertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\n//   errorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n//   if hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") { ... }\n//   assertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"not found\")\n// =============================================================================\n\n// assertErrorResponse validates that an HTTP response is an error response with\n// the expected status code and content type.\n//\n// This helper performs basic validation of error responses by checking:\n// - The HTTP status code matches the expected value\n// - The Content-Type header contains the expected type\n// - The status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx range)\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 403, 500)\n//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type substring (e.g., \"application/xml\", \"application/json\")\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tassertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\nfunc assertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedContentType string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Validate status code\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content type (allow partial match for charset parameters)\n\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, expectedContentType) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected content type to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure status code indicates an error response\n\tif resp.StatusCode < 400 || resp.StatusCode >= 600 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code %d does not indicate an error response (expected 4xx or 5xx)\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n}\n\n// parseErrorResponse extracts error details from an HTTP response body.\n//\n// This helper reads the response body and attempts to parse error details from\n// either JSON or XML format. It supports both formats for maximum flexibility:\n// - JSON: {\"code\": \"...\", \"message\": \"...\"}\n// - XML: <Error><Code>...</Code><Message>...</Message></Error>\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error details from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - errorCode: The parsed error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//   - message: The parsed error message\n//   - err: Error if parsing fails or body cannot be read\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\terrorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tif errorCode != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", errorCode)\n//\t}\n//\tt.Logf(\"Error message: %s\", message)\nfunc parseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (errorCode string, message string, err error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Read response body\n\tbody, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif readErr != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", readErr)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t// Try JSON format first\n\tvar jsonError struct {\n\t\tCode    string `json:\"code\"`\n\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\t}\n\tif jsonUnmarshalErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &jsonError); It performs a case-sensitive substring match, allowing for\n// flexible error message validation without requiring exact matches.\n//\n// This is useful for validating error message content when the exact wording\n// may vary, but key information should always be present.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedText: Text that should be present in the error message (case-sensitive)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"not found\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"does not exist\")\n//\tassertErrorMessageContains(t, resp, \"key\")\nfunc assertErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t_, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ADDITIONAL ERROR RESPONSE HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// Additional helper functions for common error response validation scenarios.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// getHTTPStatusCode extracts the HTTP status code from a response.\n//\n// This is a simple helper that returns the status code. Use it when you need\n// to check the content type in assertions or conditional logic.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract Content-Type from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - string: The Content-Type header value (empty string if not present)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tcontentType := getContentType(resp)\n//\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, \"application/xml\") {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected XML content type, got '%s'\", contentType)\n//\t}\nfunc getContentType(resp *http.Response) string {\n\treturn resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n}\n\n// getResponseBody reads and returns the response body as a string.\n//\n// This helper reads the entire response body and returns it as a string.\n// The response body is consumed in the process, so this should only be called\n// once per response. Use it for debugging or when you need to inspect the raw\n// response content.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for proper test failure reporting\n//   - resp: HTTP response to read body from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - string: The response body content\n//   - error: Error if reading the body fails\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/test-bucket/missing-key\")\n//\tbody, err := getResponseBody(t, resp)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to read response body: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tt.Logf(\"Response body: %s\", body)\nfunc getResponseBody(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (string, error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\treturn string(body), nil\n}\n\n// isErrorStatusCode checks if a status code indicates an error.\n//\n// This helper returns true if the status code is in the 4xx or 5xx range,\n// indicating an error response. It provides clear error messages\n// when validation fails, making it easier to debug test failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to validate\n//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 500)\n//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type (e.g., \"application/xml\", \"application/json\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, err := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Request failed: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tassertErrorResponse(t, resp, 404, \"application/xml\")\nfunc assertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedStatusCode int, expectedContentType string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Validate status code\n\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatusCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedStatusCode, resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate content type\n\tcontentType := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif !strings.Contains(contentType, expectedContentType) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected content type to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure status code indicates an error\n\tif resp.StatusCode < 400 || resp.StatusCode >= 600 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code %d does not indicate an error response\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n}\n\n// parseErrorResponse extracts error details from a response body.\n//\n// This helper reads the response body and attempts to parse error details\n// from either JSON or XML format. It returns the error code, message, and\n// any parsing error that occurred. This is useful for extracting structured\n// error information for further validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to extract error details from\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - errorCode: The parsed error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//   - message: The parsed error message\n//   - err: Any error that occurred during parsing\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\terrorCode, message, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n//\t}\n//\tif errorCode != \"NoSuchKey\" {\n//\t    t.Errorf(\"Expected NoSuchKey, got %s\", errorCode)\n//\t}\nfunc parseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) (errorCode string, message string, err error) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Read response body\n\tbody, readErr := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif readErr != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", readErr)\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t// Try JSON format first\n\tvar jsonError struct {\n\t\tCode    string `json:\"code\"`\n\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\t}\n\tif jsonUnmarshalErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &jsonError); jsonUnmarshalErr == nil && jsonError.Code != \"\" {\n\t\treturn jsonError.Code, jsonError.Message, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Try XML format (S3 standard)\n\tvar xmlError S3Error\n\tif xmlUnmarshalErr := xml.Unmarshal(body, &xmlError); xmlUnmarshalErr == nil && xmlError.Code != \"\" {\n\t\treturn xmlError.Code, xmlError.Message, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// If neither format works, return error\n\treturn \"\", \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response as JSON or XML (body: %s)\", string(body))\n}\n\n// hasErrorCode checks if a response contains a specific error code.\n//\n// This helper parses the error response and checks if it contains the\n// expected error code. It returns true if the error code matches, false\n// otherwise. This is useful for conditional validation and error type checking.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance (for proper test failure reporting)\n//   - resp: HTTP response to check\n//   - expectedErrorCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - bool: True if the response contains the expected error code, false otherwise\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresp, _ := MakeGETRequest(server.URL, \"/armor/blobs/missing.dat\")\n//\tif !hasErrorCode(t, resp, \"NoSuchKey\") {\n//\t    t.Error(\"Expected NoSuchKey error code\")\n//\t}\nfunc hasErrorCode(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response, expectedErrorCode string) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\terrorCode, _, err := parseErrorResponse(t, resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn errorCode == expectedErrorCode\n}\n\n// assertErrorMessageContains validates that an error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper checks that the error message in the response contains the\n// expected text.
